excerpt: neither the polytheist nor those who harbor affection or enmity toward him can penetrate the Imāmate's sanctuary; the verse indicates that they cannot even approach such a sanctuary
source_work: Rahat al-ʿAql or al-Masabih fi Ithbat al-Imama

excerpt: certain emissions invalidate ritual ablution: discharge from front or rear orifice—whether urine, feces, flatus, or semen; sleep that entirely overcomes the mind also requires renewal of ablution

The impurity of urine is greater than the impurity of feces; substances excreted from front and rear openings exceed others in severity, which is why ablution becomes obligatory solely upon their expulsion

Iblīs refused to prostrate before Ādam because he was fashioned from clay while Iblīs was created from fire; this arrogance accumulated all forms of pride within him, bringing about his fall and curse
source_location_hint: discussion of the twelve angels and the fall of Iblīs

excerpt: When a person defecates, an angel compels him to gaze upon his excrement, showing the world's true nature beneath its veil of refinement; the disbeliever's inner corruption is similarly revealed at death
source_location_hint: discourse on the illusions of worldly attachment

excerpt: The Magi of this Community are those who combine allegiance to Amīr al-Muʾminīn ʿAlī with allegiance to his opponent, just as the Magians believe in two gods and conflate light with darkness
source_location_hint: refutation of dual loyalties and theological error

excerpt: Light possesses a source—the Messenger of Allāh—and the light itself is his Successor ʿAlī; darkness possesses no source, only absence of light; oppression is analogously the absence of the Imāmate's guidance
source_location_hint: doctrine of the Source (manbaʿ) and its emanation
